blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: We're going to be okay in seventh grade.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: Because we were okay in sixth grade and we were okay in fifth grade and we were okay in fourth grade when Eli moved here and thought he was going to run everything.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: He kind of does run everything.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: He kind of does, but the point is we were okay.

The oak trees on her street were old and big enough to block the streetlights. It got darker for half a block and then lighter again, and in the darker parts the sky came through — more stars than you could see from most of Millhaven, because of the canopy.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: I don't know. Just different. People change.
He looked at her sideways in the dark. She was walking with her hands in her jacket pockets even though it wasn't actually cold enough for a jacket.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: How are you always so sure about things?

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: I'm not always sure. I'm just sure about the ones that matter.
